title: Pronoun
subtitle: SSC CGL Tier 1 + Tier 2 Complete Notes

definition.english: A word used in place of a noun is called a pronoun.
definition.marathi: Noun च्या जागी वापरल्या जाणाऱ्या शब्दाला Pronoun म्हणतात.

types.name: Personal Pronoun
types.english: Pronouns used instead of names of persons.

types.name: Possessive Pronoun
types.english: Pronouns showing possession without a noun.

types.name: Demonstrative Pronoun
types.english: Pronouns used to point out persons or things.
types.marathi: व्यक्ती किंवा वस्तू दाखवण्यासाठी वापरले जाणारे Pronouns.

types.name: Distributive Pronoun
types.english: Refers to persons/things one at a time.
types.marathi: एकावेळी एका व्यक्ती/वस्तूचा उल्लेख करणारे Pronouns.

rules.title: Order Of Pronouns (Positive Sentence)
examples: You, he and I have completed the work.

wrongRight.wrong: I, you and he have completed the work.
wrongRight.right: You, he and I have completed the work.

rules.title: Order Of Pronouns (Negative Sentence)
rules.marathi: Negative किंवा blame sentence मध्ये 1-2-3 क्रम.
examples: I, you and he are responsible for the mistake.

wrongRight.wrong: You, he and I are responsible for the mistake.
wrongRight.right: I, you and he are responsible for the mistake.

rules.title: Each / Every Rule
rules.english: After Each and Every, singular pronouns are generally used.
rules.marathi: Each / Every नंतर सामान्यतः Singular Pronoun.
examples: Each student should perform his duty.

wrongRight.wrong: Every boy must complete their work.
wrongRight.right: Every boy must complete his work.

rules.title: Preposition + Objective Case
rules.english: After a preposition, objective pronouns are used.

rules.title: Let Rule
rules.english: After Let, objective pronouns are used.

rules.title: Comparison Rule
rules.english: In formal comparisons, subjective pronouns are often preferred.
rules.marathi: तुलना करताना सामान्यतः Subjective Pronoun वापरतात.
examples: No other employee is as efficient as he.

rules.title: Apostrophe Rule (Its vs It's)
rules.english: Possessive pronouns do not take apostrophes.
rules.marathi: Possessive Pronouns मध्ये Apostrophe (') वापरत नाहीत.

rules.title: Reflexive Pronoun Agreement
rules.english: Reflexive pronouns must agree with their subjects and cannot be used as subjects alone.
rules.marathi: Reflexive Pronoun हे Subject शी जुळले पाहिजे आणि ते एकटे Subject म्हणून वापरता येत नाही.

rules.title: Collective Noun Rule
rules.english: A collective noun used as one unit takes a singular pronoun; if members act separately, use plural.
rules.marathi: Collective Noun एक गट म्हणून असेल तर Singular Pronoun, सदस्य स्वतंत्र असल्यास Plural Pronoun.

rules.title: Same Case Rule
rules.english: Pronouns joined by 'and' should remain in the same case.
rules.marathi: And ने जोडलेले Pronouns एकाच Case (Subjective/Objective) मध्ये असावेत.
examples: The teacher praised him and me.

wrongRight.wrong: The teacher praised he and I.
wrongRight.right: The teacher praised him and me.

rules.title: Who vs Whom Rule
rules.english: Who acts as subject (He/She/They), Whom acts as object (Him/Her/Them).
rules.marathi: Who चा वापर Subject म्हणून तर Whom चा वापर Object म्हणून होतो.
examples: The boy who won the race is my friend.
The man whom I met was helpful.

rules.title: That Rule (Superlative & Mixed Antecedents)
rules.english: Use 'that' after superlative degrees and when a person + thing/animal are combined.
rules.marathi: Superlative Degree नंतर आणि व्यक्ती + वस्तू/प्राणी एकत्र आल्यास 'that' वापरतात.
examples: Virat is the best player that India has produced.
The man and the dog that we saw belonged to our neighbour.

wrongRight.wrong: The man and the dog who we saw.
wrongRight.right: The man and the dog that we saw.

rules.title: That / Those Repetition Rule
rules.english: Use 'that of' for singular and 'those of' for plural nouns to avoid repetition.
rules.marathi: Noun ची पुनरावृत्ती टाळण्यासाठी Singular साठी that of आणि Plural साठी those of वापरतात.
examples: The climate of Pune is better than that of Mumbai.
The rivers of India are larger than those of America.

wrongRight.wrong: The salary of a manager is higher than a clerk.
wrongRight.right: The salary of a manager is higher than that of a clerk.

rules.title: Reciprocal Pronouns (Each other / One another)
rules.english: Use 'each other' for two entities, and 'one another' for more than two.
rules.marathi: दोन व्यक्ती/वस्तूंसाठी Each other आणि दोनपेक्षा जास्तीसाठी One another वापरतात.
examples: Ram and Shyam help each other.

wrongRight.wrong: The two sisters love one another.
wrongRight.right: The two sisters love each other.

rules.title: One -> One's Rule
rules.english: When the subject is 'One', its possessive form must be 'One's'.
rules.marathi: Subject 'One' असल्यास त्याचा Possessive Form 'One's' असावा.
examples: One must perform one's duty sincerely.

rules.title: Either of / Neither of Structural Rule
rules.english: Either of / Neither of takes a plural noun/pronoun but always requires a singular verb.
rules.marathi: Either of / Neither of नंतर Plural Noun/Pronoun येतो, पण Verb नेहमी Singular असतो.
examples: Neither of the boys is absent.

wrongRight.wrong: Neither of the boys are absent.
wrongRight.right: Neither of the boys is absent.

rules.title: It is + Subjective Case
rules.english: Pronouns following a form of the verb 'to be' (is/was/were) must be in the subjective case.
rules.marathi: To Be च्या रूपांनंतर (is/was/were) नेहमी Subjective Case वापरतात.
examples: If I were he, I would accept the offer.

wrongRight.wrong: It is me who solved the problem.
wrongRight.right: It is I who solved the problem.

rules.title: Gerund + Possessive Adjective
rules.english: Use a possessive adjective before a gerund (v+ing acting as a noun).
rules.marathi: Gerund (v+ing) च्या आधी नेहमी Possessive Adjective (my, his, your) वापरतात.
examples: He insisted on my staying there.

wrongRight.wrong: I didn't like him coming late.
wrongRight.right: I didn't like his coming late.

rules.title: But (Except) Preposition Rule
rules.english: When 'but' means 'except', it acts as a preposition and takes an objective pronoun.
rules.marathi: जेव्हा 'but' चा अर्थ 'शिवाय/खेरीज' (except) असतो, तेव्हा ते Preposition असते व त्यानंतर Objective Case येते.
